title: Backhousia leptopetala
intro: Backhousia leptopetala, commonly known as lemon myrtle, is a plant species native to Australia that belongs to the family Myrtaceae. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in the relief of colds, coughs, and sore throats. The bioactive constituents of lemon myrtle include citral, limonene, and pinene, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with lemon myrtle include teas, essential oils, and capsules, which are often used to support respiratory health and promote a sense of calm.
